ail: verb. To cause to suffer; to trouble, afflict. (Now chiefly in interrogative or indefinite constructions.)
alb: noun. A long, white robe worn by priests and other ministers, underneath most of the other vestments.
alibi: noun. The plea or mode of defense under which a person on trial for a crime proves or attempts to prove being in another place when the
all: determiner. Every individual or anything of the given class, with no exceptions (the noun or noun phrase denoting the class must be plural
bail: noun. Security, usually a sum of money, exchanged for the release of an arrested person as a guarantee of that person's appearance for
bal: noun. The surface of a mine.
ball: noun. A solid or hollow sphere, or roughly spherical mass.
bib: noun. An item of clothing for people (especially babies) tied around their neck to protect their clothes from getting dirty when eating.
biblical: adjective. Of or relating to the Bible.
bill: noun. A written list or inventory. (Now obsolete except in specific senses or set phrases; bill of lading, bill of goods, etc.)
blab: verb. To tell tales; to gossip without reserve or discretion.
cab: noun. A compartment at the front of a truck or train for the driver.
call: verb. To reach out with one's voice.
ilial: adjective. Archaic form of iliac (“Pertaining to the ilium”).
lac: noun. A resinous substance or lacquer produced mainly on the banyan tree by the female of Kerria lacca, a scale insect.
laic: noun. A layperson as opposed to a cleric, i.e. not a member of the clergy.
lilac: noun. A large shrub of the genus Syringa, especially Syringa vulgaris, bearing white, pale- pink, or purple flowers.
abb: noun. A type of yarn for the warp.
abc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ambala Ayta.
bac: noun. A broad, flat-bottomed ferryboat, usually worked by a rope.
bai: noun. A marshy meadow in sub-Saharan Africa.
bilic: adjective. Of or relating to bile.
cill: noun. Alternative form of sill (“a threshold or brink across the bottom of a canal lock for the gates to shut against”).
lai: noun. A mostly North European medieval form of lyrical, narrative poem written in octosyllabic couplets that often deals with tales of
lila: noun. The play of god’s spirit manifested in the universe; divine play, creative spiritual energy.
llb: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lolo.
